skill.name: caveman
skill.description: Ultra-compressed communication mode that reduces filler, articles, and pleasantries while preserving technical accuracy.
skill.summary: Make AI responses terse, direct, and technically complete after the user asks for caveman mode.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks and weak-crypto keywords, but the file contains only response-style instructions and examples. No executable command invocation, cryptographic implementation, network access, filesystem access, data exfiltration, or prompt-injection attempt was found.

low_findings.title: Static command findings are Markdown examples
low_findings.description: The flagged backticks appear in formatting patterns, inline examples, and a quoted SQL warning example. They are not Ruby, shell, or executable skill code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown-only and these lines are examples or quoted text. I found no shell execution API or runtime script context.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptography findings are text matches
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography hits occur in YAML metadata and a destructive-operation label. No cryptographic algorithm or hashing logic is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matching text is ordinary prose, not crypto code. No evidence supports a real weak cryptographic algorithm issue.

content.value_statement: Long assistant replies can waste tokens and slow technical work. This skill switches Claude, Codex, or Claude Code into a terse mode that keeps essential detail.
actual_capabilities: Activates a terse response style when the user requests caveman mode.
Removes filler, pleasantries, hedging, and unnecessary articles.
Preserves exact technical terms, code blocks, and quoted errors.
Uses fragments, abbreviations, and arrows to reduce response length.
Temporarily expands wording for security warnings and irreversible actions.
limitations: It changes response style only; it does not add tools or automation.
It may sound blunt in conversations that need a softer tone.
It relies on the assistant to preserve meaning while compressing language.
It must be disabled by asking for normal mode or stop caveman.

use_cases.title: Speed up debugging sessions
use_cases.description: Get compact cause, fix, and next-step summaries while keeping important technical details.

use_cases.title: Reduce token use in long chats
use_cases.description: Keep multi-turn assistance short when context size or API cost matters.

use_cases.title: Make operational answers scannable
use_cases.description: Receive direct status, risk, and action notes without greetings or long framing.

prompt_templates.title: Start terse mode
prompt_templates.prompt: Use caveman mode for this chat. Keep answers short, exact, and technical.

prompt_templates.title: Debug with compact output
prompt_templates.prompt: Use caveman mode. Explain the likely bug, the fix, and one verification step.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize a technical plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use caveman mode. Summarize the plan as problem, cause, fix, and risk.

prompt_templates.title: Keep safety details clear
prompt_templates.prompt: Use caveman mode, but expand any security warning or irreversible action confirmation.

output_examples.input: Why did the component update again?
output_examples.output: New object prop each render -> new reference -> update. Memoize prop or move object outside render.
Pool reuses DB connections. Less handshake cost. Faster requests under load. Tune size for DB limits.

output_examples.input: Should I delete this production table?
output_examples.output: Warning: destructive production action. Verify backup, export data, confirm owner approval, then proceed only if required.

best_practices: Use it for technical workflows where speed and token economy matter.
Ask for normal mode when tone, teaching detail, or nuance becomes important.
Keep safety-critical confirmations explicit even when terse mode is active.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for sensitive user support that needs empathy.
Do not compress legal, medical, or security caveats beyond clear meaning.
Do not expect it to execute commands, edit files, or add integrations.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It makes assistant replies shorter by removing filler while preserving technical content.

faq.question: Does it change code blocks?
faq.answer: No. The instructions say code blocks and exact error messages should stay unchanged.

faq.question: How do I turn it on?
faq.answer: Ask for caveman mode, talk like caveman, use caveman, be brief, or less tokens.

faq.question: How do I turn it off?
faq.answer: Ask for stop caveman or normal mode.

faq.question: Is it safe for destructive operations?
faq.answer: It includes an exception that expands warnings for irreversible actions and security-sensitive steps.

faq.question: Does it run external commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a Markdown instruction file and does not contain executable command code.
